content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Start/Stop/Transfer Service | PG&E

  - url: https://www.pge.com/en/account/service-requests/start-stop-transfer-service.html

  - source: Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E)

  - domain: pge.com


  ## Content


  # Start, stop, or transfer PG&E service


  PG&E provides account-based procedures for starting gas or electric service, stopping
  service at an existing location, and moving service to another address that PG&E
  serves.


  ## Start residential gas or electric service


  A new residential customer can begin from PG&E''s registration flow. The process
  asks the customer to identify themselves as a new PG&E customer, choose a residential
  account, enter the new service address, confirm that the meter can be accessed safely,
  choose a start date within the available scheduling window, verify identification,
  review the information, and submit the request.


  A current PG&E customer who wants to add service at another address can sign in
  to My Account, open the Start, Stop or Transfer Service area, choose the option
  to add another address, enter the new service address, confirm meter access, select
  a start date, review the request, and submit it.


  Current customers without an online account can create one using their phone number,
  email, account number, and ZIP code, or contact a PG&E customer representative.


  Some residential start-service situations cannot be completed through the ordinary
  online flow. The page directs customers to contact PG&E when the home is newly built
  and the builder has not already started service, when the customer has certain prior
  credit issues, or for most solar-customer situations outside the limited online
  exception identified by PG&E.


  ## Stop residential gas or electric service


  Customers with online access can sign in, go to the account dashboard, and open
  Start, Stop or Transfer Service. If the expected stop-service option is missing,
  the page notes that the request may already be in progress or the service may already
  have stopped.


  The page also gives separate handling for gas shutoff and restart connected with
  building fumigation. Those cases depend on safe meter access, building re-entry
  status, access to gas appliances, and advance service coordination.


  ## Move service to another address


  PG&E describes a move flow for an existing customer moving to another address served
  by PG&E. The customer can stop service at the current home and start service at
  the new home in one transaction by signing in, going to the My Account dashboard,
  and choosing Start, Stop or Transfer Service.


  The move procedure is therefore tied to a destination that is within PG&E''s service
  area. The page does not present the transfer flow as a way to move unrelated internet,
  water, sewer, or other providers'' services.


  ## Other service-account boundaries


  The page distinguishes moving an account from switching service between unrelated
  people. Except for a limited surviving-spouse situation, PG&E says service generally
  cannot simply be transferred from one person''s name to another person''s name.
  The existing customer stops service and the other person starts new service with
  their own credentials.


  For customers who need to begin or end service, PG&E''s instructions consistently
  use the service address, account context, requested service date, and the specific
  gas or electric service request. Customers can review request status from the Service
  Requests section of the online account when that status is available.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Xfinity Moving - Transfer Your Xfinity Service

  - url: https://www.xfinity.com/learn/moving

  - source: Xfinity

  - domain: xfinity.com


  ## Content


  # Moving Xfinity service


  Xfinity provides a moving flow for customers who want to keep internet and related
  Xfinity services when they change addresses.


  ## Equipment and installation


  Customers may be able to take existing equipment to the new home. When different
  equipment is needed, the moving flow may offer professional installation or self-installation
  options.


  ## Plans and availability vary by area


  The page says plan and promotional availability can differ by location. A customer
  may not always be able to keep the same plan after moving and may need to review
  offers available at the destination.


  The page also addresses moves to places where Xfinity does not provide service.
  In that situation, the customer should return Xfinity equipment instead of assuming
  the current internet service can be transferred.


  This means the generic moving process does not by itself establish that a particular
  new building can receive Xfinity service. Destination availability remains relevant.


  ## Scheduling a move


  Xfinity says a move can be scheduled in advance of the move date. Customers who
  qualify for self-installation may be able to activate service after moving in, while
  professional installation depends on appointment availability.


  If there is a gap between leaving the old address and entering the new one, the
  page describes temporary connectivity options such as eligible Xfinity WiFi hotspots
  and streaming access.


  ## After arrival


  Customers are directed to activate equipment for the new address. The page also
  provides support options for slow or unreliable service after a move.


  The moving page is a provider-specific internet continuity guide. It describes what
  may happen to equipment, plans, scheduling, and installation, but exact serviceability
  at a particular destination still depends on the new location.

  '

title: Xfinity Moving - Transfer Your Xfinity Service

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Our Methodology | How We Do What We Do | Allconnect

  - url: https://www.allconnect.com/methodology

  - source: Allconnect

  - domain: allconnect.com


  ## Content


  # How Allconnect describes its role


  Allconnect describes itself as an independent, advertising-supported publisher and
  comparison service. Its site presents information gathered from provider sources
  and other data sources so that consumers can compare home-service options.


  ## How provider availability is assembled


  For internet availability, Allconnect says it works with internet and TV providers
  and combines several kinds of data. The methodology page refers to federal broadband
  availability data, Allconnect''s own technology, and information supplied by providers.


  When a visitor enters an address, the service uses those sources to show providers
  that may be available in the area. Provider-supplied service areas and public availability
  data can be used to update the results.


  Allconnect also publishes provider ratings based on factors such as affordability,
  performance, value, and customer satisfaction. Those ratings are intended to help
  consumers compare plans and providers.


  ## Advertising and partner relationships


  The methodology page explains that Allconnect may be compensated by partners when
  users click links or sign up for services through the site. Compensation may affect
  the order in which products appear.


  The page also explains that information is shared with a selected provider during
  checkout. The stated purpose of these relationships is to connect consumers with
  providers and products.


  ## Editorial role


  Allconnect says its site does not include every provider or product in the market.
  Its editorial recommendations are based on research performed by its editorial team,
  and the page identifies outside data sources used for reporting and comparison work.


  The methodology therefore describes a marketplace, publisher, and comparison role
  built around provider information and partner relationships. Service availability,
  account ownership, installation, and the underlying service itself remain associated
  with the selected provider rather than with Allconnect as a utility, internet carrier,
  or municipal service operator.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Our Methodology | How We Do What We Do | Allconnect
url: https://www.allconnect.com/methodology

content.content:
Your move services are set up for **July 18, 2026** at:

**1818 15th Street, Sacramento, CA 95811**

- **Electricity:** New service submitted with **Sacramento Municipal Utility District (SMUD)**  
  Submission: `SUB-MOVE-1207-ELECTRICITY`
- **Gas:** Transfer submitted with **Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E)**  
  Submission: `SUB-MOVE-1207-GAS`
- **Internet:** New service submitted with **AT&T Fiber** because Xfinity is not serviceable at the new building  
  Submission: `SUB-MOVE-1207-INTERNET`
- **Water/sewer:** No tenant account was opened. These services are managed by **Riverfront Commons Property Management** through its move-in utility setup process.

The existing-service review found no blocked services. I did not submit stop/disconnection requests for the current address.
